channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris is attending church in Georgia and encouraging Black congregants to vote
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Kamala Harris is going to church in Georgia on Sunday, where she will speak to the faithful and encourage Black congregants to vote as part of a nationwide campaign push known as “souls to the polls.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In part of swing state Michigan, neighbors skip presidential signs in a heated campaign season
item.description: Nick Hannawa has more than a half-dozen election signs lining the yard around his suburban Detroit home.
item.dc:creator: Corey Williams And Mike Householder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Child labor violations on the rise, problem could get worse: report
item.description: Between 2011 and 2020, the rate of injuries among workers under 18 nearly doubled, a recent report says.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Harris eyes a rural Maine congressional district in a hunt for every possible electoral vote
item.description: LEWISTON, Maine (AP) — Rob Rogers has seen his rural corner of New England turn into a stronghold for Donald Trump in two consecutive elections. But this year he's feeling hopeful that Vice President Kamala Harris could reclaim a potentially decisive electoral vote for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Montana GOP candidate who could flip control of Senate nagged by claims he lied about bullet wound
item.description: A former Park Service ranger says U.S. Senate hopeful Tim Sheehy of Montana has been lying about a bullet wound that the candidate said came from fighting in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Home stretch: Harris, Trump hold dueling events, rallies in crucial battleground state
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Trump held dueling campaign events in battleground Michigan with less than three weeks to go until Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge unseals heavily redacted trove of evidence in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The judge overseeing Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case made public Friday a heavily redacted trove of documents that provide a small glimpse into the evidence prosecutors will present if the case ever goes to trial.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Disaster unemployment assistance available after Hurricane Milton
item.description: The Florida Commerce announced that Disaster Unemployment Assistance (DUA), is now available to businesses and residents whose employment or self-employment was lost or interrupted by Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden's gone silent on Ukraine support, ranking member of Armed Services Committee warns
item.description: Roger Wicker, the head of the Senate’s armed forces committee, urged President Biden to aggressively accelerate aid to Ukraine in his final weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP lawmakers seek investigation of consulting firm over its work in China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of Republican lawmakers is demanding a federal investigation into the U.S. consulting firm McKinsey over its work with the Chinese government and state-owned enterprises, even as the company reportedly is revamping its China business to reduce risks.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris barnstorms battleground Michigan
item.description: Vice President Harris is barnstorming the battleground of Michigan on Friday with three campaign stops in a "blue wall" state that is critical to victory on Election Day and in a place where polls show the race on a razor's edge.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Republicans raise questions after funds for disaster loans exhausted
item.description: A group of GOP senators are pressing the Small Business Administration (SBA) for answers after officials said its disaster loan program exhausted its funds earlier this week in the aftermath of a pair of major hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, Fox 5

item.title: Election 2024: How the 'New York Seven' could decide the House
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York voters have much to consider on Election Day. In addition to voting for the next president of the United States, New Yorkers will be asked to decide on a proposed amendment to the state Constitution, a U.S. Senate seat, and House representatives.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Bret Baier says he made 'a mistake' during Harris interview
item.description: Fox News anchor Bret Baier said former President Donald Trump's comments about "the enemy within" was mistakenly absent from a clip shown.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Trump yard sign leads to shooting in Las Vegas neighborhood
item.description: Two men were playing about the former president titled "[Expletive] Donald Trump" when they decided to park their car outside the pro-Trump house.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2024 election will determine who presides in Minnesota courtrooms, remember there are judges on your ballot
item.description: There are about 100 races on Minnesota’s election ballots that won’t get that much attention, and they’re all about judgeships. MPR News politics editor and his guests talk about judicial elections and why they’re different here than in other states.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Former Minnesota Congressman Rick Nolan dies at age 80
item.description: Former U.S. Rep. Rick Nolan, who represented two of Minnesota’s congressional districts in Washington more than 30 years apart, has died at the age of 80.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff and Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Barbed wire, high fences: some election spots in Arizona close, but others step up
item.description: Some election locations in Arizona are shutting their doors in the face of security threats — but others are expanding their footprint.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: At Wisconsin stops, Harris campaign features less talk of joy and more attacks on Trump
item.description: In La Crosse on Thursday, Harris told a rally crowd that Trump “will stop at nothing to claim unchecked power for himself.” It’s a shift from the “joy” that swirled around her elevation to the top of the Democratic ticket this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Candidate Karl Catarata talks education, safety, and accountability in Clark County School Board race
item.description: Karl Catarata, running for the District A trustee seat on the Clark County School Board, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide for an interview to discuss his platform and vision for the district.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois alderwoman claims she was arrested after criticizing the mayor
item.description: Alderwoman Colby Chapman said she spent five hours in jail, in what she calls a politically motivated abuse of power.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: How to watch the Nevada US Senate Debate
item.description: The only debate between Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown will occur on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump tried a friendlier pitch with Latino voters at a Univision town hall
item.description: During a town hall outside Miami, Trump touted his record on the economy, yet called Jan. 6 a "day of love" and would not back off false claims about Haitian migrants eating pets in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Johnson's chief of staff says school CEO and board kept 5th Floor in the dark
item.description: In an exclusive sitdown, Cristina Pacione-Zayas invited WGN to City Hall’s 5th floor to, in her words, “clear the air.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: The 3rd Precinct keeps showing up in the GOP playbook. Minneapolis wants to turn the page
item.description: Speaking outside the former 3rd Precinct in Minneapolis this week, Republican vice presidential candidate JD Vance criticized Gov. Tim Walz for his response to civil unrest in 2020, painting a picture of a burned-down city with rampant crime.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump would be the oldest person to become president. He’s not sharing health details
item.description: If he wins next month’s election, Donald Trump would be the oldest person in U.S. history elected president. Yet the 78-year-old Republican nominee is refusing to disclose new details about his physical or mental well-being, breaking decades of precedent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Canceling subscriptions has to be as easy as signing up, FTC says in a new rule
item.description: The "click-to-cancel" rule, now finalized by the Federal Trade Commission, aims to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and memberships. Companies argue the agency overstepped its authority.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh, MPR News

item.title: In close Minn. House races, personal problems old and new can be vexing for candidates
item.description: With the Minnesota House majority hinging on a small number of seats, flaws in candidate pasts are resurfacing as parties and groups jockey for an edge that can decide key races.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Shannon Bilbray-Axelrod discusses growth, traffic, and affordable housing in county commissioner race
item.description: Shannon Bilbray-Axelrod, a candidate for county commissioner and current Nevada Assembly member, discussed her key priorities for her district on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News
